## Structure

TCP C→S 0x8737 — `GetGamedata`. Client requests world-entry
data. Fixed 6-byte body, all-zero trailer. Verified
2026-05-10 against all 12 retail samples from 6/17 captures.

```
[0..1]   87 37                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2..5]   00 00 00 00            CONSTANT padding
```

Wire framing: `fe 06 00 87 37 00 00 00 00` (3-byte prefix +
6-byte body) = 9 bytes total on the wire.

All 12 observations are byte-identical: `87 37 00 00 00 00`.
NO content variation.

## Variants

Single 6-byte form. Pure constant probe.

## Observed contexts

Emitted twice per session (12 emissions / 6 captures) — once
right after `AuthB` succeeds, and once after the world-entry
TCP descriptors land. Triggers the server's world-entry
sequence (`Gamedata` + `UDPServerData` + `Packet830D` +
`Location`).

The 11 captures that DON'T emit this are likely C→S `0x8737`
captured by a different probe path (e.g. RESUME instead of
fresh login).

## Open questions

- The 4 trailing zero bytes: reserved for an
  install/auth-token if a future client version starts
  populating it? Currently constant.
- Why does the client emit this 2× per session? Possibly once
  per UDP-port allocation cycle (initial + after socket
  reconnection).

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.client_tcp.GetGamedata` — triggers
the world-entry response sequence:

1. `Gamedata` (TCP S→C 0x873a, 2-byte ack).
2. `UDPServerData` (TCP S→C 0x8305, 28-byte UDP descriptor).
3. `Packet830D` (TCP S→C 0x830d, 4-byte GameinfoReady).
4. `Location` (TCP S→C 0x830c, spawn zone descriptor).

The CRITICAL ordering: `UDPServerData` MUST precede
`Location`. The client's NetMgr routes 0x8305 (UDPServerData)
into queue case `\x05` which populates worldserver IP/port at
+0x2d4/+0x2d8 BEFORE 0x830c (Location) triggers the
state-machine zone-change. If Location arrives first, the
client joins a session with uninitialised memory and times out
at 15s with "Connection to worldserver failed".

The handler also adds a 200ms `DummyEvent` delay before
emission (per `GetGamedataAnswer.eventTime`).
